page: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring an AMD EPYC 9254 processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 2.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this dedicated server is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Storage is provided via a local 960 GB SSD, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates strong computational and memory performance. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng ranks in the top tier (top 10%), while multi-core performance falls within the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with large-block write operations ranking in the top 10% and read-only operations in the top 25%. Additionally, the PassMark memory score is in the top 10%, reflecting the high throughput of the 6 GHz DDR5 memory. Cryptographic and compression tasks also perform strongly, with bzip3 single-threaded decompression reaching top-tier status.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal server offers high resource density and dedicated hardware allocation, eliminating virtualization overhead. The combination of 384 GB DDR5 RAM, dedicated AMD EPYC cores, and fast local SSD storage makes this server well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd is a dedicated bare metal server powered by an AMD EPYC 9254 processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.9 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory and a local 960 GB SSD, with no GPU accelerators. The server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and memory write bandwidth, alongside strong multi-core and database execution capabilities. With its dedicated x86_64 architecture and high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this configuration eliminates virtualization overhead. It is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, static web serving, and heavy multi-threaded processing. The system provides strong memory bandwidth and high-speed cryptographic and compression performance, making it an efficient choice for resource-dense workloads.
